terminology: ACT: Active — missile has gone pitbull
HAFU: Hostile Ambiguous Friendly Unknown — radar contact symbol
L&S: Launch and Steering — primary trackfile

steps.action: Weapon Select Switch – RIGHT to select AMRAAM. Master Mode automatically sets to A/A.
steps.voiced: Press Weapon Select Switch RIGHT — select AMRAAM. Confirm A/A Master Mode.

steps.action: Set RDR ATTK page on the right DDI and SMS page on the left DDI.
steps.voiced: Set RDR ATTK on the right DDI and SMS on the left.

steps.action: On SMS page, set expected target SIZE and RCS (SMALL / MEDIUM / LARGE).
steps.voiced: On the SMS page, set target size and RCS.

steps.action: Press Sensor Control Switch RIGHT to slave the TDC to the RDR ATTK page. Confirm Select Focus Diamond appears on the radar DDI.
steps.voiced: Press Sensor Control Switch RIGHT — confirm TDC diamond on the radar screen.

steps.action: Slew TDC to the mode indicator on the RDR ATTK page (reads RWS or VS). A mode pop-up appears showing RWS / VS / TWS. Slew TDC to TWS and depress to select. Note: entering TWS drops azimuth to a maximum of 80° — 120° is not available in TWS.
steps.voiced: Slew TDC to the mode indicator and select TWS. Azimuth will drop to 80° max on entry.

steps.action: Set scan volume. 80° azimuth / 2 bars gives the widest picture. 40° / 4 bars gives faster trackfile refresh on maneuvering contacts at closer range. Narrowing the scan helps hold trackfiles in a turning fight.
steps.voiced: Set scan volume — 80 and 2 bar for a wide picture, 40 and 4 bar for tighter refresh on close maneuvering threats.

steps.action: Adjust the Antenna Elevation wheel to center the scan volume on the expected threat altitude.
steps.voiced: Adjust antenna elevation to center on the threat altitude.

steps.action: Confirm AUTO scan centering is active (default on TWS entry). In AUTO, azimuth and elevation follow the L&S trackfile automatically. If the display shows MAN, press the AUTO OSB on the RDR ATTK page.
steps.voiced: Confirm AUTO scan centering is active — scan follows L&S automatically.

steps.action: Slew TDC over the first threat HAFU symbol. TDC DEPRESS – designates that contact as L&S (primary target). This is a soft designation, not an STT lock — the radar continues to scan. Confirm a square TD box appears on the HUD.
steps.voiced: Slew TDC to the first threat and press TDC DEPRESS — that’s your L&S. Confirm square on HUD. Radar stays in search.

steps.action: Slew TDC over the second threat HAFU symbol. TDC DEPRESS – designates that contact as DT2 (secondary target). Confirm an X symbol appears on the HUD for DT2.
steps.voiced: Slew TDC to the second threat and press TDC DEPRESS — that’s your DT2. Confirm X on HUD.

steps.action: Fly to place the Steering Dot inside the ASE / NIRD circle on the HUD for the L&S. Monitor the HUD for the SHOOT cue above the L&S TD box. TWS requires active maneuvering to reach the LAR — do not go STT, the radar must stay in search to uplink to both missiles.
steps.voiced: Fly the Steering Dot into the ASE circle for L&S. Wait for the SHOOT cue. Stay in TWS — do not push to STT.

steps.action: When SHOOT cue appears over the L&S TD box, squeeze and hold the trigger to launch missile 1. Call Fox Three. The radar remains in TWS and continues scanning — uplink to missile 1 is active. Do NOT promote L&S to STT; doing so will break uplink to a second missile.
steps.voiced: SHOOT cue — squeeze trigger. Fox Three. Radar stays in TWS, uplink active. Do not go STT.

steps.action: Press the Undesignate Button once. This swaps L&S and DT2: the previous DT2 becomes the new L&S, and the old L&S becomes DT2. Verify the TD box on the HUD has moved to the second target.
steps.voiced: Press Undesignate Button once to swap L&S and DT2. Confirm TD box has moved to target 2.

steps.action: Fly to place the Steering Dot inside the ASE / NIRD circle for the new L&S (target 2). Wait for SHOOT cue.
steps.voiced: Fly Steering Dot into the ASE circle for the new L&S. Wait for SHOOT cue.

steps.action: When SHOOT cue appears, squeeze and hold the trigger to launch missile 2. Call Fox Three. The radar is now uplinking to both missiles simultaneously while remaining in search.
steps.voiced: SHOOT cue — squeeze trigger. Fox Three. Radar is now uplinking to both missiles.

steps.action: Monitor the ACT timers on the HUD. When a missile’s ACT timer reaches zero, it has gone pitbull and is self-guiding — uplink from the radar is no longer required for that missile. Once both missiles are pitbull, you may crank, beam, or notch as tactically appropriate.
steps.voiced: Watch the ACT timers. When each missile hits pitbull, uplink drops — crank or notch as needed.
